note: C-06/C-08 capability inventory. The lethal-trifecta rule (C-08): no session may hold all three of {untrusted-input, private-data, external-communication}. Checked mechanically by tests/test_security_invariants.py on every gate run. Adding a capability that completes the triangle fails the build.

sessions.name: llm-advisor
sessions.rationale: Receives TradingView indicators + pairwise summaries (treat as untrusted). Returns schema-validated JSON only. Has NO tools, NO outbound channel of its own, and never handles credentials. It cannot act, send, or exfiltrate.

sessions.name: trade-engine
sessions.rationale: Holds the operator's keys (private-data) and reaches the exchange/SMS (external-communication). It acts ONLY on schema-validated LLM output through the capped _swap chokepoint; there is no path by which untrusted text becomes an instruction here. The [untrusted-input] leg is absent.
